purpose: Track whether Complex mechanisms are stable core, validated, tested, candidate, or retired so public docs do not present fresh patches as proven behavior.

status_meanings.core: Stable behavior spine or boundary that every Complex run should respect.
status_meanings.validated: Backed by repeated real transcripts or end-to-end project samples plus behavior cases and examples.
status_meanings.tested: Covered by behavior cases, transcript rules, examples, or repeated user feedback, but not yet validated by a broad transcript set.
status_meanings.candidate: Promising mechanism from recent failures or external calibration; use when relevant, but do not present as proven.
status_meanings.retired: No longer active; kept only if needed to explain a replacement.

promotion_requirements.candidate_to_tested: Has behavior case coverage, transcript rule coverage, at least one filled example or template landing point, and external calibration when mechanism-level.
promotion_requirements.tested_to_validated: Has real transcript or end-to-end project evidence showing lower correction cost or better forward progress across at least two relevant runs.
promotion_requirements.validated_to_core: Is broadly applicable, low-friction, repeatedly useful, and cannot be handled by cases/examples alone.
promotion_requirements.demotion: Repeated transcript failures, high overhead, obsolete Codex surface semantics, or evidence that the mechanism causes local-greedy or governance-drag behavior.

mechanisms.name: Complex behavior kernel
mechanisms.summary: Seven stable behaviors that compress every Complex run into state recovery, project nature, decision rights, control plane, target-function execution, delivery, and recovery.
external_basis: OpenAI AGENTS.md guidance for durable repository instructions
internal_evidence: Central in README, quickstart, protocol/core, current-state, and skill
Covered by setup_card_default and multiple orchestration cases
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Already core because it is the compression layer for all other mechanisms, not a narrow recent patch.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If repeated real transcripts show agents follow mechanism names while missing the seven behaviors, simplify the kernel further.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Any change to the public entrypoint, skill, or protocol core.

mechanisms.name: Codex surface alignment
mechanisms.summary: Maps Plan mode, Codex Goal, beat_objective, subagents, standing lanes, threads, worktrees, automations, AGENTS.md, skills, and tools to their Codex-native meanings.
internal_evidence: Behavior cases cover Plan mode, Goal semantics, subagent boundaries, standing lanes, and source resolution.
Skill and README use Codex-native terms.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote to core only after real transcripts show fewer Plan/Goal/thread misunderstandings across at least two target projects.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If Codex surfaces change or transcript failures show the mapping causes worse behavior.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Codex app, Goal, subagent, automation, skill, or AGENTS.md documentation changes.

mechanisms.name: Project nature router
mechanisms.summary: Classifies work as evidence_fill, model_discovery, mixed, or execution_delivery so evidence-heavy and discovery-heavy projects do not use the same weights.
internal_evidence: Covered by model_discovery and evidence_fill cases and examples.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote if end-to-end project samples show fewer early-convergence and over-divergence errors.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If agents over-label mixed projects and stall execution.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When a new project class repeatedly does not fit the four natures.

mechanisms.name: Prompt bootstrap and rehydration
mechanisms.summary: Designs a project prompt before execution and refreshes each beat from the master prompt, current state, target function, and beat_objective.
internal_evidence: Covered by prompt_bootstrap_first and continuous_cadence_rehydrate.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after real transcripts show follow-on plans preserve the master plan across multiple beats.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If rehydration creates visible reporting burden or repeats stale goals.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: After any prompt default or Codex planning surface change.

mechanisms.name: Continuous cadence contract
mechanisms.summary: Same-run queued beats continue inside the responsibility boundary until STOP_COMPLETE or a true boundary, not a fixed beat count.
external_basis: OpenAI Codex Automations for cross-turn heartbeat when available
Agent workflow patterns for routing and evaluator loops
internal_evidence: Behavior cases cover rehydration, runtime activation after plan, same-turn vs heartbeat, and known-next-step auto execution.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after real transcripts show sustained auto-progression without repeated user continue prompts.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If agents run past real responsibility boundaries or produce low-value beat churn.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When Codex automation/thread heartbeat tooling changes.

mechanisms.name: Responsibility boundary
mechanisms.summary: AI handles project-internal decisions; the user is asked only for responsibility-bearing boundaries such as goal changes, accounts, payment, publishing, external writes, irreversible actions, public voice, high-risk claims, or external commitments.
external_basis: OpenAI Agents SDK guardrails and human review concepts
NIST AI RMF governance accountability framing
internal_evidence: Repeated user feedback identified over-asking as a major failure; cases cover API guardrails, downstream boundary reconciliation, and responsibility wording.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Already core because it defines autonomy and user authority across all Complex runs.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If transcripts show safety overreach or under-asking for true external commitments.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Any new tool class with external side effects or user-owned commitments.

mechanisms.name: Human intervention drift guard
mechanisms.summary: Prevents unnecessary user work, manual summaries, and permission-seeking when the next project-internal beat is clear.
external_basis: SRE toil reduction for avoiding unnecessary human labor
internal_evidence: Covered by known_next_step_auto_execute and context_pointer_first_intake.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after real transcripts show fewer unnecessary user prompts across at least two repositories.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If agents skip needed user authority or hide important uncertainty.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Whenever user feedback reports over-asking or under-asking.

mechanisms.name: Independent review context separation
mechanisms.summary: Distinguishes same-session diagnostic review from true independent review using clean context, fact ledgers, separate reviewers, or read-only audit workers.
external_basis: OpenAI Subagents for bounded read-only workers
Research review norms around blinded or independent review
internal_evidence: Covered by independent_review_context_separation and the independent_review example.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after real review transcripts show cleaner critique and fewer inherited-chat biases.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If independent review overhead exceeds project value for routine beats.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When review lane or subagent tooling changes.

mechanisms.name: Operating organization
mechanisms.summary: Long projects establish controller, human interface, literature/data, model/component, data-code, review/risk, and writing/delivery lanes before local optimization.
external_basis: Team Topologies responsibility and cognitive-load concepts
internal_evidence: Recent user feedback strongly supports it; behavior cases and examples exist but real transcript validation is pending.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote to tested after two real projects show lane contracts reduce local-greedy drift.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If agents build lane records instead of moving the project.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When long-project practice reveals a recurring missing lane or excessive topology overhead.

mechanisms.name: Portfolio control plane
mechanisms.summary: Manages target function, modules, standing lanes, forward indexes, branch parking, routing, calibration, sentinel status, and stop conditions before choosing local beats.
internal_evidence: Covered by portfolio_before_local_greedy_route, audit guardrail, hot/warm/cold, and portfolio example; real transcript validation pending.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after end-to-end samples show multiple modules move without losing mainline.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If portfolio bookkeeping becomes a substitute for execution.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: After repeated local-greedy failures or module/lane expansion.

mechanisms.name: Target-function Loop
mechanisms.summary: Replaces nearby-lightest action with the smallest meaningful learning or execution cycle serving the project target function and producing a forward artifact.
internal_evidence: Covered by loop_not_local_greedy and examples, but newly reframed.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after real beats show fewer local-greedy loops and more forward artifacts.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If loop paperwork slows simple execution-delivery tasks.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When loops repeatedly fail to move a closure segment or artifact.

mechanisms.name: Forward artifact acceptance
mechanisms.summary: A beat is accepted only when it creates or updates a forward artifact, passes a guard, updates state/indexes, and selects a route.
external_basis: NASA success criteria and technical baselines
internal_evidence: Behavior cases cover audit guardrails and portfolio routing; examples include forward artifacts.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after real transcripts show STOP_COMPLETE and next routes are based on artifact progress rather than guardrail-only work.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If agents force artificial artifacts for genuinely diagnostic beats.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When accepted beats are later judged low value.

mechanisms.name: Trace appraisal and Hot/Warm/Cold state
mechanisms.summary: Keeps auditability while demoting stale trace from hot context into compact indexes or cold archives by pointer.
internal_evidence: Covered by hot_warm_cold_state_lightening and trace_appraisal cases.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after long project samples show lower context load without losing recovery.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If compaction hides current basis or breaks handoff recovery.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When Hot State length or user feedback indicates context pollution.

mechanisms.name: External calibration rule
mechanisms.summary: Strategic route, structure, model, method, evaluation, prompt-default, or protocol changes compare against mature external practice and become micro-contracts.
internal_evidence: Covered by external_calibration cases and external calibration example.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after repeated changes include external basis and avoid self-referential protocol fixes.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If agents perform superficial framework naming without transfer boundaries.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Every mechanism-level change or stale external basis.

mechanisms.name: Hallucination sentinel
mechanisms.summary: Separates current basis, external basis, inference, unsupported claim, and falsification cue at startup, phase switches, public delivery, external calibration, and before core promotion.
internal_evidence: Present in protocol, quickstart, templates, and examples; still needs real transcript outcome validation.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after review records show unsupported claims are downgraded before delivery.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If sentinel becomes rote text with no route change.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: At public delivery, claim upgrade, external calibration, or repeated self-reference.

mechanisms.name: Attention governance and minimum viable closure
mechanisms.summary: Uses minimum sufficient observability and early thin closure to prevent audit/reporting/process work from consuming project movement.
internal_evidence: Recent user feedback; behavior cases and examples exist but real end-to-end validation is pending.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after real projects show earlier question-source/model-result-claim chains and less reporting overhead.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If closure language causes premature convergence or fake slices.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When many beats pass without a visible chain or when reporting burden increases.

mechanisms.name: Human-readable delivery contract
mechanisms.summary: Aligns audience, purpose, granularity, tone, and internal-information boundary; human-facing output avoids machine fields and protocol jargon unless decision-relevant.
external_basis: OpenAI prompting guidance for clear task outputs
internal_evidence: Longstanding user feedback and behavior case coverage.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Already core because every Complex run ends in an audience-specific deliverable or recovery note.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If human outputs become too sparse for project decisions.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Whenever user requests a new audience type or delivery style.

mechanisms.name: Behavior transcript review
mechanisms.summary: Checks real agent replies against behavior-case marker groups and human review questions, without claiming marker pass equals excellence.
external_basis: Anthropic agent evals emphasizing trajectories and outcomes
internal_evidence: 36 cases and 36 transcript rules validate structurally; real transcript outcome dataset remains small.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after a corpus of real transcripts links marker pass to lower correction cost.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If marker pass regularly disagrees with human judgment.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: After every real transcript review batch.

mechanisms.name: Example currentness review
mechanisms.summary: Filled examples stay current only if they state validated mechanisms, unverified boundaries, applicable project types, and non-copy conditions.
external_basis: OpenAI Skills examples and progressive disclosure
internal_evidence: New review needed; current examples are filled examples, not externally certified examples.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote examples to certified representative cases only after real transcript or end-to-end project reuse.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If an example teaches obsolete terms or causes agents to overfit form.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: After terminology changes, protocol promotion, or new project class.

mechanisms.name: Source resolution and runtime install
mechanisms.summary: Complex is the rule source and a target project is the fact source; missing Complex files inside the target repository is not a failure.
internal_evidence: Behavior case covers target repo source confusion; README provides install path.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Promote after public users apply Complex from another repo without source confusion.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If source-resolution wording becomes noisy or defensive.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: When install model changes.
